events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario is about roofing work where nail placement is "high/exposed" - meaning nails are placed too high on the shingle or exposed rather than being covered by the overlapping course above. This is a common roofing defect.

Nail placement high/exposed means the fasteners are not properly placed per manufacturer specifications and IRC R905. Exposed nails can lead to water infiltration, and high placement doesn't properly secure the shingle. This is non-compliant.

Decision: fail
Risk: medium (water infiltration potential)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: maybe 70-80% since roughed in but needs rework
Confidence: high, say 0.9
